executiveSummary: This SaaS Agreement has been reviewed by Synth in mock mode. The document contains several clauses that warrant attention, including payment terms, termination provisions, and liability limitations. This is a demonstration analysis — no actual AI analysis was performed. Review all findings with a qualified professional.
paymentTerms: $4,500 per month (the "Subscription Fee"), billed annually in advance
renewalTerms: automatically renew for successive one-year periods (each a "Renewal Term") unless Customer provides written notice of cancellation at least ninety (90) days prior to the end of the then-current term
terminationTerms: Either party may terminate this Agreement for material breach upon sixty (60) days written notice if the breach is not cured within such period
liabilityIssues: IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, EXEMPLARY, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES, INCLUDING LOSS OF PROFITS, DATA, OR BUSINESS OPPORTUNITIES, EVEN IF PROVIDER HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POS
indemnificationIssues: Indemnification provisions present. Review scope and carve-outs with legal counsel.
confidentialityTerms: Each party agrees to maintain the confidentiality of the other party's Confidential Information and not to disclose such information to any third party without prior written consent
governingLaw: governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to conflict of l
unusualClauses: Unilateral modification rights may allow changes without consent

topRisks.title: Unilateral Modification Rights
topRisks.explanation: Provider may be able to modify terms without customer consent.
topRisks.whyItMatters: Material terms could change after signing without recourse.
topRisks.suggestedNextStep: Request a mutual written amendment requirement.
topRisks.supportingQuote: automatically renew for successive one-year periods (each a "Renewal Term") unless Customer provides written notice of cancellation at least ninety (90) days prior to the end of the then-current term

topRisks.title: Limited Liability Cap
topRisks.explanation: Liability may be capped at a small fraction of potential damages.
topRisks.whyItMatters: Company may have no meaningful recourse for serious failures.
topRisks.suggestedNextStep: Negotiate a higher cap and carve-outs for willful misconduct.
topRisks.supportingQuote: IN NO EVENT SHALL PROVIDER BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, EXEMPLARY, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES, INCLUDING LOSS OF PROFITS, DATA, OR BUSINESS OPPORTUNITIES, EVEN IF PROVIDER HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POS

topRisks.title: Auto-Renewal with Short Notice
topRisks.explanation: Agreement auto-renews unless cancelled within a specific window.
topRisks.whyItMatters: Missing the notice window locks in another full term.
topRisks.supportingQuote: automatically renew for successive one-year periods (each a "Renewal Term") unless Customer provides written notice of cancellation at least ninety (90) days prior to the end of the then-current term

actionItems: Review all payment and fee terms with finance team
Calendar all notice and cancellation deadlines
Have legal counsel review liability and indemnification provisions
Request data processing agreement if applicable
Verify governing law is acceptable for your jurisdiction

citations.quote: This analysis was generated in mock mode. No AI provider was used.
citations.relevance: All findings are illustrative. Run with a real provider for document-specific analysis.

disclaimer: Synth is not legal advice or financial advice. It is a document review aid. Consult a qualified professional before making decisions.
